message:
fix(runtime,command): ACL security fixes — privilege escalation and resetpass

Security fixes:
- Deleted user sessions now denied access (was: granted full unrestricted
  access, allowing privilege escalation after ACL DELUSER)
- SWAPDB command table entry: add admin+dangerous flags (was: write+fast
  only, allowing restricted users to swap entire databases)

Behavioral fixes:
- ACL SETUSER resetpass: no longer sets enabled=false. In Redis, resetpass
  only clears passwords without disabling the user. Use "off" to disable.
- ACL DRYRUN: check permissions only (full_access), not enabled status.
  DRYRUN evaluates "would this be allowed?" not "can this user auth?"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -8548,7 +8548,7 @@ const COMMAND_TABLE: &[(&str, i64, &str, i64, i64, i64)] = &[
     ("bgsave", -1, "admin", 0, 0, 0),
     ("bgrewriteaof", 1, "admin", 0, 0, 0),
     ("lastsave", 1, "fast", 0, 0, 0),
-    ("swapdb", 3, "write fast", 0, 0, 0),
+    ("swapdb", 3, "write fast admin dangerous", 0, 0, 0),
     ("object", -2, "readonly", 2, 2, 1),
     ("memory", -2, "readonly", 0, 0, 0),
     ("slowlog", -2, "admin", 0, 0, 0),
